symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent fluttering / skipped-beat sensation at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The skip-then-thump description at rest is typical of premature ectopic beats with a compensatory pause.
Flutter sensations can occur in SVT; character alone does not distinguish.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heightened awareness of the heartbeat (somatic fixation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Interoceptive amplification is characteristic of anxiety-driven symptom fixation.

symptoms.symptom_name: High caffeine load (~5 energy/caffeinated drinks today)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stimulant load is a well-established reversible precipitant of atrial and ventricular ectopy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute high psychological stress / severe anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic surge both generates ectopy and amplifies its perception.
Absence of chest pain lowers (does not eliminate) concern for ischaemia as a driver.
Palpitations without syncope are a strong negative for a life-threatening arrhythmia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sustained tachyarrhythmia with compromise / heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of dyspnoea argues against a decompensating sustained arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations intermittent and brief, not sustained/racing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A non-sustained, self-terminating pattern is more typical of ectopy than a sustained re-entrant tachycardia.
Brief, self-limited episodes fit isolated ectopy.
Exertionally triggered palpitations are more concerning for structural/ischaemic substrate; rest-only ectopy is more often benign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-reported heart rate ~84 bpm, mostly regular, on a smartwatch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A mostly-regular normal rate on a consumer wearable argues weakly against AF/SVT but cannot exclude paroxysms; unverified patient-obtainable data.

symptoms.symptom_name: Auscultation: regular rate and rhythm with occasional premature beats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Occasional premature beats on a regular background is the auscultatory correlate of benign ectopy — clinician-measured, sealed; the AI cannot auscultate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal S1/S2 without murmurs, gallops or rubs; no peripheral oedema; strong symmetric pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A structurally normal exam argues against a structural substrate — clinician-measured, sealed.
Absence of goitre weakly argues against thyroid disease; TFTs are definitive — clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal 12-lead ECG (isolated ectopics, no sustained arrhythmia)

investigation_details.expected_result: Sinus rhythm with occasional atrial/ventricular ectopics; no AF, no pre-excitation, no ischaemic changes, normal intervals
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without a documenting ECG the rhythm is never characterised and AF/SVT/pre-excitation cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Documents benign ectopy and excludes the dangerous mimics — the pivotal investigation, unavailable via telehealth.
Only a rhythm recording can confirm or exclude AF.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Thyrotoxicosis as a driver of palpitations would be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal TFTs exclude a hyperadrenergic thyroid cause.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Brief, at-rest, non-sustained flutters with a large caffeine load and acute stress, and no exertional trigger, cluster strongly toward benign precipitated ectopy.
No chest pain, no syncope, no dyspnoea, not sustained — the combination that lets a clinician step down from emergency concern to a prompt documented GP work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Documents the rhythm, confirms benign ectopy and excludes AF/SVT/pre-excitation/ischaemia. Cannot be performed in telehealth chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests, FBC and electrolytes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes thyrotoxicosis and electrolyte disturbance as reversible drivers of ectopy.
Reserved for recurrent or persistent symptoms where a resting ECG is normal but a paroxysmal arrhythmia must be captured.
No chest pain, no ischaemic features and haemodynamically stable — troponin is not indicated and risks a false-positive cascade.
Not first-line for isolated benign ectopy with a normal ECG and exam; reserve for an abnormal ECG, a murmur, or frequent/complex ectopy on monitoring.
